journal_entry.description: Borrowed $200,000 from bank at 6% annual interest for 3 years, interest payable semi-annually
journal_entry.rationale: Cash received increases asset (debit). Loan creates a liability (credit). The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one asset increase line, and credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one liability increase line. The $200,000 principal amount is the loan payable; interest will be accrued and expensed in future periods when incurred. This is a straightforward borrowing transaction with no tax implications (loan proceeds are not taxable income, and interest expense will be recorded when incurred per accrual accounting).

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines receives $200,000 cash from the bank. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset. This is the only debit line in this borrowing transaction. The loan liability and future interest payments are credit-side entries (not classified here).

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines receives $200,000 cash from the bank, which creates a loan obligation. On the credit side: the bank loan represents a liability increase (the company now owes $200,000 to the bank). The interest rate and payment terms affect future transactions but do not create additional credit lines at the time of borrowing.
